that this asymmetrically doish reaction
function is not just a US phenomenon
it's a global phenomenon because there's
way too much sovereign debt globally and
somebody's got to buy it and these guys
are trying to entice us to buy it
because they don't have enough air cover
to expand their balance sheets like they
would in a recession or like they would
in a significant downturn in asset
markets so now they just got to ease
policy with inflation way too high and
then it's it's a tight line to dance
because if you do too much easing with
inflation High then the bond market
yields are bond yields are going to run
away from you so they got to it's a real
tight rope that these these guys are
doing and I quite

there there's no easy solutions you
either have inflation Financial
repression or monetary monetary
debasement or financial repression or

can do and you need to be aware of that

has been confirmed and buying here once

research is not set up for that the

that you're going to sell the exact top
and buy the exact bottom nobody knows
